title: Queued Agent Prompt Dispatch
interaction_flow: Agent → Ai: recordPrompt(new QueuedAgentPrompt(agent, prompt, attachments, provider, model))
Agent → FakePendingDispatch: new FakePendingDispatch() — no-op constructor, no actual dispatch
Agent → InvokeAgent: dispatch(agent, prompt, attachments, provider, model) — returns PendingDispatch
InvokeAgent → Queue: push job onto Laravel queue
Agent → Caller: return QueuedAgentResponse (wrapping real PendingDispatch)
key_relationships: Promptable is a PHP trait mixed into Agent classes
FakePendingDispatch overrides all fluent methods (onConnection, onQueue, delay, chain, afterCommit, etc.) as no-ops returning $this
FakePendingDispatch.__destruct() is empty — prevents PendingDispatch parent from actually dispatching
FakePendingDispatch.getJob() returns anonymous empty class (no real job)
QueuedAgentPrompt is a plain DTO: holds agent, prompt, attachments, provider (Lab|array|string|null), model (?string)
Ai::recordPrompt() stores QueuedAgentPrompt for later test assertions
QueuedAgentResponse wraps either FakePendingDispatch or real PendingDispatch
branching_conditions: Agent::isFaked() (Ai::hasFakeGatewayFor) → true: test/fake path; false: production dispatch path
broadcastOnQueue() follows identical branching but dispatches BroadcastAgent instead of InvokeAgent
important_notes: FakePendingDispatch neutralises the Laravel PendingDispatch destructor trick (dispatch-on-destruct) so no real queue push occurs in tests. Ai::recordPrompt() enables assertQueued()/assertNotQueued()/assertNeverQueued() assertions on the Promptable trait. The QueuedAgentPrompt DTO is only created on the fake path; on the real path, payload is passed directly to InvokeAgent::dispatch().
